symptoms.symptom_name: Unilateral anterior nasal bleeding after nose-blowing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unilateral bleed with local trauma trigger is typical anterior epistaxis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bleeding controlled by correct anterior pressure; no ongoing throat flow

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Control with anterior pressure and no throat flow argue strongly against a posterior source.

symptoms.symptom_name: No anticoagulant use / no bruising or petechiae (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No anticoagulant/bleeding history argues against a coagulopathic cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Excoriated bleeding point at Kiesselbach's plexus (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A visible anterior septal bleeding point confirms anterior epistaxis but requires rhinoscopy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not needed for a controlled first anterior bleed without anticoagulation/coagulopathy.
Only for recurrent bleeds, anticoagulation, or suspected bleeding disorder.
